title: Modular Robot Workshop
subtitle: From reconfigurable hardware to robust autonomy: methods and systems for modular robots in real environments.
location: IROS 2026 main venue (room to be announced)
overview: This workshop targets a specific question: how do we build modular robots that move beyond lab demos into reliable operation? We focus on methods that connect morphology, planning, control, and learning under realistic uncertainty, hardware limits, and deployment constraints.

focusAreas.title: Embodiment and Morphology
focusAreas.description: Design principles for self-reconfigurable structures, docking interfaces, and morphology-aware dynamics.

focusAreas.title: Planning and Control
focusAreas.description: Task-level and motion-level methods that adapt to changing robot topology and environment geometry.

focusAreas.title: Learning and Generalization
focusAreas.description: Policies and representations that transfer across module counts, layouts, and task families.

focusAreas.title: Systems and Deployment
focusAreas.description: Benchmarking, reproducibility, safety, and industrial or fielded case studies.

photos.alt: Modular robot blocks shown in a lab environment.
photos.title: Modular Robot Example
photos.description: Example of a modular robot platform demonstrating reconfigurable hardware concepts.

photos.alt: Conference scene from an IROS-related robotics event.
photos.title: IROS-Related Conference Scene
photos.description: Reference conference image for the IROS 2026 workshop page context.

cfpIntro: We invite contributions with clear technical scope and practical relevance. Both early-stage ideas and mature systems are welcome if methods and limitations are explicitly described.

submissionTypes.note: Should state problem, approach, and expected evaluation.

submissionTypes.requirement: 1 page plus a 2-3 minute video link.
submissionTypes.note: Include hardware setup, space needs, and safety notes.
Must identify open challenges and concrete future directions.

reviewCriteria: Relevance to modular robotics and workshop scope
Novelty, insight, or practical deployment value
Quality of evaluation plan or empirical evidence
Potential to stimulate discussion across communities
